name: hak5-wifi-coconut
title: hak5-wifi-coconut

commands.name: wifi_coconut

arguments.description: Go fully dark; don't enable any LEDs
arguments.raw: --disable-leds        Go fully dark; don't enable any LEDs

arguments.description: Normally a Wi-Fi Coconut enables all the LEDs
arguments.raw: --invert-leds         Normally a Wi-Fi Coconut enables all the LEDs

arguments.description: Disable blinking the LEDs on traffic
arguments.raw: --disable-blinking    Disable blinking the LEDs on traffic

arguments.description: Don't display channel UI while logging
arguments.raw: --no-display          Don't display channel UI while logging

arguments.description: Wait for a coconut to be found
arguments.raw: --wait                Wait for a coconut to be found

arguments.description: Log packets to a pcap file.  If file is '-',
arguments.raw: --pcap=[fname]        Log packets to a pcap file.  If file is '-',

arguments.description: List Wi-Fi Coconut devices and exit
arguments.raw: --list-coconuts       List Wi-Fi Coconut devices and exit

arguments.description: If you have multiple Wi-Fi Coconuts, specify
arguments.raw: --coconut-device=X    If you have multiple Wi-Fi Coconuts, specify

arguments.description: Enable a Wi-Fi Coconut even if not all the
arguments.raw: --enable-partial      Enable a Wi-Fi Coconut even if not all the

arguments.description: Log plain 802.11 packets instead of radiotap
arguments.raw: --plain-dot11         Log plain 802.11 packets instead of radiotap

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# wifi_coconut -h
 Wi-Fi Coconut
 Usage: wifi_coconut [options]
 By default, the wifi_coconut tool opens in interactive mode.
 Universal options:
--disable-leds        Go fully dark; don't enable any LEDs
--invert-leds         Normally a Wi-Fi Coconut enables all the LEDs
                      and blinks during traffic; Invert only lights
                      when there is traffic.
--disable-blinking    Disable blinking the LEDs on traffic
 Non-interactive modes:
--no-display          Don't display channel UI while logging
--wait                Wait for a coconut to be found
--pcap=[fname]        Log packets to a pcap file.  If file is '-',
                      a pcap file will be echoed to stdout so that it can
                      be piped to other tools. --wait-for-coconut    Wait for a coconut to be connected and identified
--list-coconuts       List Wi-Fi Coconut devices and exit
--coconut-device=X    If you have multiple Wi-Fi Coconuts, specify
                      which one to use
--enable-partial      Enable a Wi-Fi Coconut even if not all the
                      radios have been identified.
--plain-dot11         Log plain 802.11 packets instead of radiotap
                      formatted packets with signal and channel
--quiet               Disable most output

apt_metadata.description:
Userspace driver for the Hak5 Wi-Fi Coconut
Userspace drive for USB Wi-Fi NICs and the Hak5 Wi-Fi Coconut
